person.name: Sophia von Wittelsbach

burialPlace.address: Kloster Reinhardsbrunn, Gothar ,  Hzgt Thüringen, HRR

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Sophia of Wittelsbach (1170 - 1238) was a daughter of Otto I Wittelsbach, who was Count Palatine and later Duke of Bavaria, and his wife Agnes of Loon. In 1196, Sophia married Landgrave Hermann I of Thuringia; she was his second wife. «b»They had the following children:«/b» 1.) Irmgard (b. 1197), married in 1211 to Count Henry I of Anhalt 2.) Louis IV (1200 - 1227) 3.) Herman (1202 - 1216) 4.) Conrad (1204 - 1247), grand master of the Teutonic Knights 5.) Henry Raspe (1204 - 1247) 6.) Agnes, married twice: in 1225 to Henry "the Profane" of Babenberg (1208 - 1228), a son of Margrave Leopold IV of Austria in 1229 to Duke Albert I of Saxony (c.?1175 - 1261)

Theodora Angelina ( 22/23 June 1246) was the wife of Leopold VI of Austria, by whom she had several children. As a child, she was used by her grandfather, Emperor Alexios III Angelos, as a political tool to gain the allegiance of the regional strongmen Ivanko and Dobromir Chrysos. Her betrothals or marriages to them were cut short as both were successively captured by the Emperor.

«b»Identity«/b»
The identity of Leopold VI's wife has long been disputed by scholars. The medieval sources only recorded her name, Theodora, and that she was the granddaughter of a Byzantine emperor (Theodoram neptam regis Graecorum duxit uxorem, Theodoram neptem regis Grecie). Modern scholars, beginning with Georg Juritsch in 1894, have offered different opinions on who that emperor was, with some considering Theodora the granddaughter of Isaac II Angelos (r. 1185-1195, 1203-204), while others held that it was Alexios III Angelos (r. 1195-1203). Still others interpreted the neptis not as granddaughter as in Classical Latin, but as niece, or even great-niece, which was the more common meaning of the word in the Middle Ages.

She has now been conclusively identified as the daughter of Isaac Komnenos Vatatzes, the grandson of the Byzantine general Theodore Vatatzes and the purple-born princess Eudokia Komnene, daughter of Emperor John II Komnenos (r. 1118-1143), and of Anna Komnene Angelina, the second daughter of Emperor Alexios III Angelos).

«b»Life«/b»
Her parents married in or slightly before 1190, and Theodora was born shortly after, since in 1203 she was just entering her fourteenth year.

«b»Betrothal to Ivanko and Dobromir Chrysos«/b»
Her father was promoted to the rank of sebastokrator when Alexios III acceded to the throne in 1195, but in the summer of the same year, he was captured during a campaign against Bulgarian rebels under Asen, and imprisoned in the Bulgarian capital, Tarnovo, where he died in spring 1196.

According to one story relayed by the contemporary chronicler Niketas Choniates, Isaac played a role in the assassination of Asen by the disaffected Bulgarian boyar Ivanko: Isaac reportedly promised Ivanko the hand of Theodora, in exchange for the deed. Isaac died before Asen's murder, but after Ivanko killed Asen and fled to the Byzantine court, where he entered Imperial service, Emperor Alexios III betrothed him to Theodora to cement his allegiance. Due to her young age, Choniates describes the bride-to-be as "a child mouthing baby talk", the marriage, if there ever was one, was never consummated; Theodora remained in Constantinople, while Ivanko rose in revolt in 1199, only to be captured and imprisoned by a ruse.

Shortly after, Theodora was used by Alexios III to gain the allegiance of another regional Balkan warlord, Dobromir Chrysos, who in 1201 had risen in revolt along with the disaffected Byzantine general Manuel Kamytzes. Chrysos was already married to Kamytzes' daughter, but readily accepted the Emperor's offer, and surrendered the fortresses of Pelagonia (modern Bitola) and Prilep, that he had captured, to the Emperor's forces. Shortly after, Alexios III turned on Chrysos as well, and took him prisoner.

«b»Marriage to Leopold VI of Austria and offspring«/b»
In 1203, Theodora married a distant cousin, Leopold VI, Duke of Austria. Her husband died in 1230 and she subsequently became a nun at Klosterneuburg Monastery. She died there in June 1246, reportedly of grief for the recent death of her son, Frederick II, in battle against the Hungarians.

«b»Leopold and Theodora had seven children:«/b»
1.) Margaret, Duchess of Austria (1204 - February 28, 1266)

2.) Agnes of Austria (February 19, 1205 - August 29, 1226)

3.) Leopold of Austria (1207-216) fell from a tree and died

4.) Henry II, Duke of Modling (1208 - November 28, 1228)

5.) Gertrude of Austria (1210-1241)

6.) Frederick II, Duke of Austria (25 April 1211 - June 15, 1246)

7.) Constantia of Austria (April 6, 1212 - June 5, 1243)

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sophia von Wittelsbach於1170年出生在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 凱拉海姆時，她的父親Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern40歲，母親Agnes van Loon28歲。她和Hermann I. von Thüringen至少有4個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1238年7月10日在德國格洛斯黑爾左圖姆 薩辛-維馬-森亞賀享年68歲，埋葬在德國賀林根藍德可萊斯 格塔福利德利可洛達萊音哈斯布盧恩。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Sophia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek word meaning ‘wisdom’. The Eastern cult of  St Sophia  arose as a result of misinterpretation of the phrase Hagia Sophia ‘holy wisdom’ as if it meant ‘St Sophia’. The name became popular in England in the 17th and 18th centuries—the heroine of  Fielding's  novel Tom Jones (  1749 ) is called  Sophia Weston—and  has been increasing in popularity since the 1990s. In Scotland it has been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Beathag .

en.lifeSummary: When Sophia von Wittelsbach was born in 1170, in Kelheim, Bavaria, Germany, her father, Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern, was 40 and her mother, Agnes van Loon, was 28. She had at least 4 sons and 3 daughters with Hermann I. von Thüringen. She died on 10 July 1238, in Saxe-Weimar-Eisenach, Germany, at the age of 68, and was buried in Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Gotha, Thuringia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sophia von Wittelsbach est née en 1170, à Landkreis Kelheim, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Agnes van Loon, avait 28 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles avec Hermann I. von Thüringen. Elle est décédée le 10 juillet 1238, à Großherzogtum Sachsen-Weimar-Eisenach, Allemagne, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterrée à Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Landkreis Gotha, Thüringen, Allemagne.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sophia von Wittelsbach è nata il nel 1170 nel Landkreis Kelheim, Bayern, Germania, sua padre, Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Agnes van Loon, aveva 28 anni. Lei avuto almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie con Hermann I. von Thüringen. Morì il 10 luglio 1238, nel Großherzogtum Sachsen-Weimar-Eisenach, Germania, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolta a Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Landkreis Gotha, Thüringen, Germania.

'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayernは40歳，母Agnes van Loonは28歳であった。彼女はHermann I. von Thüringenとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1238年7月10日にドイツグロssハーゾグトゥム サチセン-ウエイマル-エイセナチで68歳で死去し，ドイツスuリンジェンランドクレイス ゴサフリードリチロダレインハードスブルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sophia von Wittelsbach(이)가 1170년 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 켈하임에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes van Loon(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 Hermann I. von Thüringen(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1238년 7월 10일 독일 그로ss헤어즈그투 작센-위마-이센악h에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 스u링 랜드크레이스 가스아 프리드리크로다 레인하르드스브루에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sophia von Wittelsbach nasceu em 1170, em Landkreis Kelheim, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Agnes van Loon, tinha 28 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas com Hermann I. von Thüringen. Ela faleceu em 10 de julho de 1238, em Großherzogtum Sachsen-Weimar-Eisenach, Alemanha, com 68 anos, e foi sepultada em Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Landkreis Gotha, Thüringen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sophia von Wittelsbach родилась в 1170 г. в местности ландкрис келхим, беиирн, Германия, её отцу по имени Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes van Loon, 28. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери от Hermann I. von Thüringen. Она умерла 10 июля 1238 г. в местности гроssхирзогтум сачсен-вимар-исенач, Германия в возрасте 68 лет и была похоронена в местности ринардсбрунн, фриедричрода, ландкрис гота, тuринген, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sophia von Wittelsbach nació en 1170, en Landkreis Kelheim, Bayern, Alemania, su papá, Otto I. 'der Rotkopf' von Wittelsbach Herzog von Bayern, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Agnes van Loon, tenía 28 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas con Hermann I. von Thüringen. Murió el 10 de julio de 1238, en Großherzogtum Sachsen-Weimar-Eisenach, Alemania, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultada en Reinhardsbrunn, Friedrichroda, Landkreis Gotha, Thüringen, Alemania.
